title: Run a Command Inside a Container
description:
## Exec Into a Running Container

A container named **`exectest`** (image `alpine`) is already **running** in the background.

**Your task:** use `docker exec` to create the file **`/tmp/ready`** inside it, containing the text **`ok`**.

> 💡 `docker exec <container> <command>` runs a command in an already-running container. To write a file you can run a shell: `docker exec exectest sh -c 'echo ok > /tmp/ready'`.

hints.title: Write a file via exec
hints.body: Run a shell inside the container with -c so redirection happens inside the container, not on the host.

hints.title: Verify from inside
hints.body: Read the file back to confirm its contents.

validation.description: Checks that /tmp/ready inside the exectest container contains 'ok'.
